title: Connecting to the host interface while sandoxed is not possible
body:
### Issue Description

I have a internal veth to which I am binding a number of services (podman rootless containers run by their own unprivileged users). These services are only expected to talk to each other or to receive requests throught the veth. The latter works as expected, albeit the former does not. I have been reading about pasta and seems to be related to how the network namespace is set up, but. I have an openldap server bound to a port on that veth and I am trying to connect from another container (connecting from the host works as expected), unsuccessfully.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue (steps from 2 onwards just fail with "ldap_sasl_bind(SIMPLE): Can't contact LDAP server (-1)" )
1. Set up the other rootless container (in my case a openldap server)
2. podman run --rm --dns=10.255.255.1 --network=pasta:--outbound-if4,cluster_dns0 --add-host=ldap.host.internal:host-gateway test_ldap sh -c "ip add && ip route && ldapwhoami -H ldaps://ldap.host.internal:1636"
3. podman run --rm --dns=10.255.255.1 --network=pasta:--outbound-if4=cluster_dns0,--gateway=10.255.255.1 --add-host=ldap.host.internal:host-gateway sh -c "ip add && ip route && ldapwhoami -H ldaps://ldap.host.internal:1636"
4. podman run --rm --dns=10.255.255.1 --network=host --add-host=ldap.host.internal:host-gateway sh -c "ip add && ip route && ldapwhoami -H ldaps://ldap.host.internal:1636"


### Describe the results you received

Just "ldap_sasl_bind(SIMPLE): Can't contact LDAP server (-1)" meaning podman cannot reach that container

### Describe the results you expected

The connection can be established

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I am closing this because the problem was related to the application: openldap client was reporting it could not connect to the server, but the problem was related to the TLS certificate.
